Pie Charts

Source: vignettes/pies.Rmd
pies.Rmd

Overview

Pie charts express portions of a whole using arcs or angles within a circle. They are ideal for showing proportions and making relative shares easy to compare at a glance. Each slice represents a category’s contribution to the total.

Setup 

library(muiCharts)
library(dplyr)

Prepare Data

For pie charts, the data must have label and value columns:

# Count characters by sex
starwars_sex <- dplyr::starwars |>
  count(sex) |>
  filter(!is.na(sex)) |>
  select(label = sex, value = n)

# Count characters by species (top 5)
starwars_species <- dplyr::starwars |>
  count(species) |>
  filter(!is.na(species)) |>
  arrange(desc(n)) |>
  slice_head(n = 5) |>
  select(label = species, value = n)

Basic Pie Chart

A simple pie chart showing the distribution of characters by sex:

PieChart(
  series = list(
    list(
      data = starwars_sex
    )
  ),
  height = 300
)

Per-Slice Colors

Add a color column to the data to assign colors to individual slices:

starwars_sex_colored <- starwars_sex |>
  mutate(color = c("#440154FF", "#2A788EFF", "#FDE725FF", "#35B779FF"))

PieChart(
  series = list(
    list(
      data = starwars_sex_colored
    )
  ),
  height = 300
)

Donut Chart

Create a donut chart by setting the innerRadius:

PieChart(
  series = list(
    list(
      data = starwars_sex,
      innerRadius = 50,
      outerRadius = 100
    )
  ),
  height = 300
)

Pie Chart with Arc Labels

Display values directly on the slices:

PieChart(
  series = list(
    list(
      data = starwars_sex,
      arcLabel = "label",
      innerRadius = 50,
      outerRadius = 100
    )
  ),
  height = 300
)

Pie Chart with Value Labels

Show the value on each arc:

PieChart(
  series = list(
    list(
      data = starwars_species,
      arcLabel = "value",
      innerRadius = 40,
      outerRadius = 100
    )
  ),
  height = 300
)

Pie Chart with Percentage Labels

Display custom formatted labels using JavaScript:

# Calculate percentages
starwars_sex_pct <- starwars_sex |>
  mutate(
    pct = round(value / sum(value) * 100, 1),
    pct_label = paste0(pct, "%")
  )

PieChart(
  series = list(
    list(
      data = starwars_sex_pct,
      arcLabel = JS("(item) => `${item.label}: ${item.pct}%`"),
      arcLabelMinAngle = 20,
      innerRadius = 50,
      outerRadius = 100
    )
  ),
  height = 300
)

Pie Chart with Padding

Add spacing between slices using paddingAngle:

PieChart(
  series = list(
    list(
      data = starwars_sex,
      paddingAngle = 5,
      cornerRadius = 5,
      innerRadius = 40,
      outerRadius = 100
    )
  ),
  height = 300
)

Semi-Circle Pie Chart

Create a semi-circle chart by setting start and end angles:

PieChart(
  series = list(
    list(
      data = starwars_sex,
      startAngle = -90,
      endAngle = 90,
      innerRadius = 50,
      outerRadius = 100
    )
  ),
  height = 250
)

Two-Level Pie Chart

Create nested pie charts with multiple series:

# Prepare inner and outer data
starwars_inner <- dplyr::starwars |>
  count(sex) |>
  filter(!is.na(sex)) |>
  select(label = sex, value = n)

starwars_outer <- dplyr::starwars |>
  filter(!is.na(sex), !is.na(species)) |>
  count(species) |>
  arrange(desc(n)) |>
  slice_head(n = 8) |>
  select(label = species, value = n)

PieChart(
  series = list(
    list(
      data = starwars_inner,
      innerRadius = 0,
      outerRadius = 60
    ),
    list(
      data = starwars_outer,
      innerRadius = 80,
      outerRadius = 100
    )
  ),
  height = 300
)

Pie Chart with Custom Size

Control the position and size of the pie:

PieChart(
  series = list(
    list(
      data = starwars_sex,
      cx = 150,
      cy = 150,
      innerRadius = 40,
      outerRadius = 80
    )
  ),
  height = 300,
  width = 300
)

Gauge-Style Donut

Create a gauge-like visualization:

# Calculate percentage for a specific category
starwars_gauge <- dplyr::starwars |>
  count(sex) |>
  filter(!is.na(sex)) |>
  mutate(
    prop = n / sum(n) * 100,
    prop_label = paste0(round(prop), "%")
  ) |>
  filter(sex == "female") |>
  select(label = sex, value = prop)

# Add remaining to complete the circle
starwars_gauge_data <- bind_rows(
  starwars_gauge,
  list(label = "Other", value = 100 - starwars_gauge$value)
)

PieChart(
  series = list(
    list(
      data = starwars_gauge_data,
      innerRadius = 70,
      outerRadius = 90,
      startAngle = -90,
      endAngle = 90
    )
  ),
  height = 250
)

Pie Chart Composition

Build pie charts from individual sub-components using ChartDataProvider. Use PiePlot to render both arcs and labels together:

ChartDataProvider(
  series = list(
    list(
      type = "pie",
      data = starwars_sex,
      innerRadius = 50,
      outerRadius = 100
    )
  ),
  height = 300,
  ChartsTooltip(trigger = "item"),
  ChartsSurface(
    PiePlot()
  )
)

Use PieArcPlot and PieArcLabelPlot separately for independent control over arcs and labels:

ChartDataProvider(
  series = list(
    list(
      type = "pie",
      data = starwars_sex,
      innerRadius = 50,
      outerRadius = 100,
      arcLabel = "label"
    )
  ),
  height = 300,
  ChartsTooltip(trigger = "item"),
  ChartsSurface(
    PieArcPlot(),
    PieArcLabelPlot()
  )
)

PieArc and PieArcLabel are the slot sub-components for individual arc and label rendering:

PieArc(skipAnimation = TRUE)
PieArcLabel()

Learn More

  • MUI Pie Charts Documentation

See also: Highlighting for hover effects, Styling for colors, Legend for legend options.
